function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---

### 1. Par Value, Authorized, Issued, and Outstanding Shares
*   **Par Values:** Class A, Class B, and Class C common stock all have a par value of **$0.001** per share. Class D common stock has a par value of **$0.0001** per share.
*   **Authorized Shares (as of March 31, 2026):** The Company is authorized to issue 54,657 million shares of common stock, allocated as follows:

    *   **Class B:** 6,125 million shares (6,125,000,000 shares post-IPO)
    *   **Class C:** 10,000 million shares (10,000,000,000 shares post-IPO)
    *   **Class D:** 2,400 million shares
*   **Issued and Outstanding Shares (Actual):**
    *   **Class A:** 2,964,501,353 shares issued and 2,882,444,444 shares outstanding.
    *   **Class B:** 2,421,276,530 shares issued and outstanding.
    *   **Class C:** 494,026,445 shares issued and outstanding.
    *   **Class D:** No shares issued or outstanding.

### 2. Changes Resulting from the 2026 Stock Split
*   **Split Mechanics:** In May 2026, the Board approved and effected a five-for-one (5-for-1) forward stock split (the "2026 Stock Split"). Each share of Class A, Class B, and Class C common stock issued and outstanding was split into five shares of the same class. 
*   **Par Value:** The par value of the common stock remained unchanged at $0.001 per share for Classes A, B, and C (and $0.0001 for Class D) following the split.
*   **Authorized Share Count:** The authorized share counts were adjusted. For example, prior to the split (as of December 31, 2025), the authorized shares were 53,855 million total (36,130 million Class A, 5,325 million Class B, 10,000 million Class C, and 2,400 million Class D). Following the split (as of March 31, 2026), the authorized total increased to 54,657 million (36,132 million Class A, 6,125 million Class B, 10,000 million Class C, and 2,400 million Class D).
*   **Retroactive Adjustment:** All share and per share information has been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 2026 Stock Split for all periods presented. The conversion rate of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was also proportionately adjusted.

### 3. Stock Options, RSUs, ESPP, and Other Equity Awards
*   **Types of Awards:** The Company grants Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), Restricted Stock Awards (RSAs), non-statutory stock options, and performance-based awards to eligible employees, key executives, and non-employee service providers. 
*   **Employee Stock Purchase Plans (ESPP):** The Company offers an ESPP allowing eligible employees to purchase common stock at a discounted price, and a Non-Qualified Employee Stock Purchase Plan (NQ ESPP) allowing purchases at fair market value (the NQ ESPP was cancelled in April 2026).

    *   27.0 million shares remained available under the ESPP.
    *   4.8 million shares remained available under the NQ ESPP.

Additionally, activities related to the Company’s Plans are presented on an as-converted basis per the xAI Merger, where each xAI option, RSU, and RSA is presented as 0.1433 SpaceX options/RSUs/RSAs. 
*   **Reserved Unissued Shares (as of March 31, 2026):** The Company reserves shares of common stock for future issuance upon the vesting/settlement of outstanding equity awards. This includes 8 shares of Class A, 450 shares of Class B, and 476 shares of Class C for outstanding stock options; and 49 shares of Class A, 1 share of Class B, and 79 shares of Class C for outstanding RSUs. An additional 150 Class A shares and 350 Class C shares are reserved for future grants under share-based compensation plans.

# SpaceX 5-for-1 Stock Split Analysis

## 1. Split-Adjusted Equivalent Price Calculation

**Pre-split Forge Global peak price (April 1, 2026):** $634.00 per share
**Split ratio:** 5-for-1 forward stock split (effective May 4, 2026)
**Split adjustment factor:** 1/5 = 0.20

**Split-adjusted equivalent price per share:**
$634.00 ÷ 5 = **$126.80 per share**

This represents the economic equivalent of one post-split share. The total enterprise value attributable to a holder is unchanged; only the per-share unit of measurement has been divided by five.

---

## 2. Specific Financial Statement Line Items and Per-Share Disclosures Affected by the Retroactive Adjustment

The S-1 explicitly states: *"Unless otherwise stated or the context otherwise requires, all share and per share information included in this prospectus have been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 2026 Stock Split."* The following specific items are affected:

### A. Consolidated Statements of Operations (Income Statement)
- **Net loss/income per share of common stock attributable to common shareholders — Basic and Diluted** (e.g., $(1.27) for Q1 2026; $(1.69) for FY 2025; $0.01 for FY 2024; $(1.68) for FY 2023)
- **Weighted average shares used in computing net loss/income per share — Basic and Diluted** (e.g., 3,884 million for Q1 2026; 2,926 million for FY 2025; 2,848 million for FY 2024; 2,759 million for FY 2023)

### B. Summary Historical Consolidated Financial and Operating Data
- All per-share and weighted-average-share figures presented in the summary table (mirroring the income statement figures above)

### C. Unaudited Pro Forma Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share
- **Weighted average shares used in computing net loss per share** (3,884 million for Q1 2026; 2,926 million for FY 2025)
- **Pro forma adjustment to reflect the Preferred Conversion** (6,723 million shares for both periods)
- **Weighted average shares used in computing pro forma net loss per share** (10,607 million for Q1 2026; 9,649 million for FY 2025)
- **Pro forma net loss per share of common stock — Basic and Diluted** ($(0.41) for Q1 2026; $(0.51) for FY 2025)

### D. Earnings Per Share Note Reconciliation
- **Weighted average shares of common stock outstanding — Basic and Diluted**
- **Loss per share attributable to common shareholders — Basic and Diluted**
- **Anti-dilutive securities excluded from the diluted per-share calculation** (xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock: 1,369 / 537 million; SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock: 6,733 / 6,780 million; share-based compensation: 623 / 18 / 767 million)

### E. Consolidated Balance Sheet — Shareholders' Equity
- **Authorized shares** of Class A, Class B, and Class C common stock (Class D was not subject to the split). Authorized total increased from 53,855 million (as of Dec 31, 2025) to 54,657 million (as of March 31, 2026)
- **Issued and outstanding shares** of Class A (2,964,501,353 issued / 2,882,444,444 outstanding), Class B (2,421,276,530), and Class C (494,026,445)
- **Par value remained unchanged** at $0.001 per share for Classes A, B, and C (and $0.0001 for Class D) — par value is not adjusted in a forward stock split

### F. Preferred Stock Conversion Rate
- **SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock conversion rate** was proportionately adjusted to **50 shares of common stock per preferred share** (post-split), up from the implied 10 shares pre-split
- The Qualified IPO auto-conversion threshold ($6.0 billion pre-public offering market capitalization; $250 million minimum proceeds) is unaffected in dollar terms

### G. Equity Awards (Stock Options, RSUs, RSAs, ESPP)
- **Shares available for future grants** under the Plans (543.8 million), ESPP (27.0 million), and NQ ESPP (4.8 million) as of December 31, 2025
- **Reserved unissued shares** for outstanding stock options and RSUs (Class A, B, and C)
- **Weighted-average exercise prices** of outstanding stock options
- All xAI-originated options/RSUs/RSAs presented on an as-converted basis (0.1433 SpaceX shares per xAI award, on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis — i.e., effectively ~0.7165 post-split)

### H. Cover Page and Capitalization
- **Shares of Class A common stock offered** in the IPO
- **Class A and Class B shares outstanding immediately before and after the offering**
- **Pro forma capitalization table** share counts
- **Underwriters' over-allotment option** share count

### I. Beneficial Ownership Table
- Pre-offering ownership based on **6,932,508,000 Class A** and **5,602,790,410 Class B** shares outstanding as of May 1, 2026, after giving effect to the Class C Reclassification, the Preferred Conversion, and the 2026 Stock Split
- Post-offering ownership percentages and share counts

### J. Dilution Section
- **Pro forma net tangible book value per share**
- **Dilution per share to new investors**
- Sensitivity disclosures (each $1.00 change in IPO price; each change in shares offered)

### K. Use of Proceeds
- Sensitivity of net proceeds to per-share IPO price changes

### L. Auditor's Report
- The auditor's report date is bifurcated: March 30, 2026 for the base opinion, with an explanatory paragraph dated **May 7, 2026** specifically addressing the effects of the common-control reorganization and the stock split discussed in Note 1

---

## 3. Why an Analyst Must Apply the Adjustment Factor Before Drawing Valuation Conclusions

An analyst comparing pre-split secondary market transaction prices (such as the Forge Global $634 peak on April 1, 2026) to post-filing per-share metrics in the S-1 must divide the pre-split price by 5 (yielding $126.80) before any meaningful comparison. The reasons are:

### (a) Unit-of-measure mismatch
The $634 Forge Global price is denominated in **pre-split shares**. Every per-share figure in the S-1 — net loss per share, pro forma net loss per share, weighted-average share counts, net tangible book value per share, and the IPO price range — is denominated in **post-split shares**. Comparing $634 to a post-split per-share metric without adjustment conflates two different units of measurement; the resulting ratio would be overstated by a factor of 5.

### (b) Market capitalization and valuation multiple errors
If an analyst multiplied the unadjusted $634 by the post-split share count (e.g., the ~12.5 billion Class A + Class B shares outstanding as of May 1, 2026), the implied equity value would be **5x overstated** — roughly $7.9 trillion instead of the economically correct ~$1.6 trillion. Any P/E, EV/EBITDA, or price-to-sales multiple derived from such a calculation would be similarly distorted, leading to materially wrong conclusions about relative valuation versus peers.

### (c) Dilution and ownership analysis errors
The beneficial ownership table, the pro forma capitalization table, and the dilution section all present share counts on a post-split basis. An analyst who fails to adjust pre-split transaction prices would miscalculate:
- Pre-IPO vs. post-IPO ownership percentages
- Dilution to new investors
- The economic impact of the Preferred Conversion and Class C Reclassification

### (d) Preferred stock and equity-award conversion errors
The SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock conversion rate was reset from an implied 10 shares to 50 shares per preferred share. The xAI preferred conversion ratios (0.1433 shares per preferred share) are explicitly disclosed "on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis." An analyst who ignores the split factor would misstate the dilutive impact of these conversions on the fully diluted share count and on pro forma EPS.

### (e) Consistency with the filing's own presentation
The S-1 itself imposes the adjustment: it states that "all share and per share information included in this prospectus have been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 2026 Stock Split for all periods presented." An analyst who does not apply the same 1/5 factor to external pre-split price observations is comparing apples to oranges — the filing's historical per-share data has been re-based, but external secondary-market prices quoted before May 4, 2026 have not. The adjustment factor is the bridge that restores comparability.

### (f) Avoiding spurious "discount" or "premium" conclusions
Without adjustment, an analyst might conclude that the IPO price (post-split) represents a deep discount to the $634 secondary-market peak — when in fact the economically equivalent post-split comparison price is $126.80. Conversely, comparing post-split per-share book value or EPS to an unadjusted $634 would produce a falsely elevated multiple. Either error could lead to incorrect buy/sell/hold recommendations or mispriced IPO demand.

---

## Summary Table

| Item | Pre-Split Value | Adjustment | Post-Split Equivalent |
|---|---|---|---|
| Forge Global peak price (Apr 1, 2026) | $634.00 / share | ÷ 5 | **$126.80 / share** |
| SpaceX preferred conversion rate | 10 shares/preferred share | × 5 | 50 shares/preferred share |
| xAI preferred → SpaceX conversion ratio | 0.1433 shares (pre-split basis) | × 5 | ~0.7165 shares |
| xAI Series A cash election price | $75.46 / share (pre-split basis) | ÷ 5 | ~$15.09 / share |
| Par value (Class A/B/C) | $0.001 | unchanged | $0.001 |
